parts.answer: An answer that makes reference to the following point: έΑλ concentration would be less and because the mass (of sodium hydroxide) would include the moisture Allow water for moisture Do not award refence to substances other than sodium hydroxide Do not award reference to the volume increases (1) Number Additional Guidance Mark έΑλ 9.51 έΑΥ 9.55 (g) Allow 9.51 ~ 9.55 (g) Allow 9.53 ┬▒0.02 (g) (1) Number . Additional Guidance Mark έΑλ calculation of molar mass of borax έΑλ calculation of moles of borax weighed out έΑλ calculation of moles of borax in pipetted 25.0 cm (1) (1) (1) Example of calculation Molar mass = 381.2 (g mol έΙΤ1 ) n = (9.53 ├╖ 381.2 =) 0.025 / 2.5 x 10 έΙΤ2 (mol) n = (0.025 x (25 ├╖ 500 =) 0.00125 / 1.25 x 10 έΙΤ3 (mol) Ignore SF except 1 SF Correct answer with no working scores (3) TE throughout (3) Number Additional Guidance Mark An answer that makes reference to the following point: έΑλ (from) yellow) (to) orange Do not award any other colours (1) Number Additional Guidance Mark έΑλ calculation of moles of HCl έΑλ calculation of concentration of HCl in mol dm έΙΤ3 έΑλ calculation of concentration of HCl in g dm έΙΤ3 to 2/3SF (1) (1) (1) Example of calculation n = (0.00125 x 2 =) 0.0025 / 2.50 x 10 έΙΤ3 (mol) c = (0.0025 ├╖ (16.30 ├╖ 1000 =) 0.15337 / 1.5337 x 10 έΙΤ1 c = (0.15337 x 36.5 = 5.5982 =) 5.60/5.6 Correct answer with no working scores (3) Allow calculations done in any order, including in one step. TE throughout and from 3(b)(i) Common wrong answers: Using 0.00125 as the moles of HCl gives 2.80/2.8 (scores 2) Dividing 0.00125 by 2 gives 1.40 /1.4 (scores 2) Using the mean of 3 titres (16.53) gives 5.52/5.5 (scores 2) (3) (Total for Question 3 = 9 marks) Number Additional Guidance Mark έΑλ HCl Ignore state symbols Ignore name Do not award if more than one answer given. (1) Number Additional Guidance Mark έΑλ carbon dioxide / CO If name and formula given then both must be correct Ignore state symbols Do not award if given with other substances (1) Number Additional Guidance Mark έΑλ

An answer that makes reference to the following points: (bromine water) έΑλ from brown/ orange / yellow έΑλ to colourless (BenedictέΑβs / FehlingέΑβs reagent) έΑλ from blue (solution) έΑλ to (brick) red precipitate (1) (1) (1) (1) Standalone marks Ignore layers throughout Do not award red Allow decolourises Ignore clear Do not award reference to effervescence Allow red-brown for red Allow solid / ppt(e) for precipitate Do not award brown / orange / pink for red Ignore reference to the solution turning colourless before the precipitate is formed Do not award reference to the solution turning white and the precipitate is formed Do not award silver mirror. (4) (Total for Question 4 = 11 marks) Number Additional Guidance Mark An answer that makes reference to the following points: έΑλ use of gloves έΑλ use a fume cupboard / hood (1) (1) Allow ensure laboratory is well-ventilated Ignore masks, safety specs, goggles, lab coat Ignore flammability Do not award using a lower concentration or less HCl (2) Number Additional Guidance Mark An answer that makes reference to the following point: έΑλ pressure / gas / CO must be released Allow to prevent the build up of pressure Ignore reference to explosions Ignore just a gas is produced Do not award reference to release air / water vapour / other gases Do not award a reference to removal of lower / aqueous layer (1) Number Additional Guidance Mark An answer that makes reference to the following point: έΑλ cloudy to clear Allow milky for cloudy Ignore just έΑαgoes clearέΑβ Ignore reference to colourless (1) Number Additional Guidance Mark An answer that makes reference to the following points: έΑλ (bp1) round-bottomed/pear-shaped flask έΑλ (bp2) heat έΑλ (bp3) still head with no gaps to outside and no blockages έΑλ (bp4) still head with entry to condenser έΑλ (bp5) thermometer with any part of bulb or bottom of thermometer opposite entry to condenser έΑλ (bp6) labelling (of condenser) with water in and water out έΑλ (bp7) condenser with exit for distillate or vent for

An answer that makes reference to three of the following points: έΑλ rate is inversely proportional to time έΑλ 2-chloro-2-methylpropane is tertiary and rate (of hydrolysis) is fastest/faster έΑλ 1-chloro-2-methylpropane and 1-bromo-2-methylpropane are primary (halogenoalkanes) and C-Br bonds are hydrolysed / broken faster / faster rate than C-Cl bonds (1) (1) (1) Allow any indication that a shorter time means a faster rate Ignore just restatement of times from the table without comment Allow greatest / higher for fastest Allow reference to shorter time Allow reference to shorter time Ignore reference to stability of carbocations Ignore references to bond strength of C-Br bonds and C-Cl bonds Allow reference to shorter time to hydrolyse C-Br bonds Accept reverse arguments throughout (3) (Total for Question 5 = 11 marks) TOTAL FOR PAPER = 50 MARKS
